_meta.description: Find all active users who are missing a personal/recovery email. Recovery email is not visible in the people list table — this payload iterates through each active user's detail page and checks whether the 'Personal / recovery email' field is populated. Returns a list of users missing it.
notes: Recovery email is labeled 'Personal / recovery email' on the onboard form and person detail edit form.
It is NOT a column in the people list table — iteration through detail pages is required.
This payload uses for_each_row to visit each active user's detail page.
With 28 active users and ~3s per detail page, expect ~90s total runtime.
The column-picker button (mdi-view-column-outline) may support adding a recovery-email column — not yet confirmed. If confirmed, simplify to a single perceive step.

items-per-page-select.resolveHint: The items-per-page dropdown in the table footer. Currently shows '10'. Click to open and select the highest available option (e.g. 100 or All).

recovery-email-field.resolveHint: On the person detail page, find the field labeled 'Personal / recovery email'. It appears in the Details tab of the person profile. May require clicking 'Edit' to reveal. Look for input with label containing 'personal', 'recovery', or 'backup'.

chain.note: Start with active users only — deactivated users missing recovery email are less actionable.
Open items-per-page dropdown to load all users on one page.
Select 100 items per page (or 'All' if available). This loads all ~28 active users without pagination.
Wait for table to reload with expanded item count.
Capture all active user rows (name, email). This list drives the iteration in s6.

sub_chain.note: Click the person's row to navigate to their detail page.
Read the personal/recovery email field value. Empty string or null means it's missing.
Return to people list for next iteration.

chain.note: Iterate each active person. Navigate to detail, check recovery email field, record if empty, return to list.
Return list of active users with no recovery email. Each entry: { name, email }.
